_meta.description: Deterministic, source-attributed Pakistan-specific process data for outbound students. Used by services/pakistan_policy_service.py and injected into route plans by services/route_plan_service.py. No AI input — every fact here is verified to a public official source URL listed in `official_source_url`.
_meta.audience: Pakistani students applying to UK / Canada / Germany student pathways.

processes.name: HEC Degree Attestation
processes.description: The Higher Education Commission of Pakistan attests Pakistani higher education degrees (Bachelor's, Master's, MPhil, PhD) and transcripts. Most foreign universities and visa services require an HEC-attested copy of your highest qualification before they accept it.
steps: Get an online appointment via the HEC eServices portal
Attend in person at an HEC regional office (Islamabad, Lahore, Karachi, Peshawar, Quetta) on the appointment day
Submit documents and pay the prescribed fee
Collect attested documents on the date specified or via courier
processes.when_required: Before submitting your final scholarship/admission package and before visa application.
processes.notes: Walk-in service is sometimes available but appointments are strongly recommended. Allow extra time during exam-clearance season (typically July–September).

processes.name: IBCC Equivalence / Attestation
processes.description: The Inter Boards Committee of Chairmen issues equivalence certificates for school-level qualifications (Matric, FA/FSc, O/A Levels). This is required when your prior education was outside the Pakistani board system or when an institution requests an equivalence certificate.
steps: Apply online via the IBCC website
Submit originals at an IBCC office (Islamabad / Karachi / Lahore / Peshawar / Quetta / Muzaffarabad)
Pay the equivalence fee
Collect the equivalence certificate
processes.when_required: Only required if you studied O/A Levels, IB, or an equivalent foreign system, OR if a university or visa office specifically asks for equivalence.
processes.notes: Not every applicant needs IBCC. Confirm whether your university or destination requires it before scheduling.

processes.name: MOFA Attestation (Apostille / Authentication)
processes.description: The Ministry of Foreign Affairs (Pakistan) authenticates HEC- and IBCC-attested documents for use abroad. Some destination countries or specific document chains require this final step before consular legalisation.
requirements: HEC-attested degree (or IBCC-attested certificate) — must be done first
steps: Book an online appointment via the MOFA Camp Office portal
Attend the relevant Camp Office (Islamabad / Karachi / Lahore / Peshawar / Quetta)
Pay the attestation fee
Collect attested documents
processes.when_required: Required for Germany when documents will be presented to German authorities; consult your destination's legalisation chain. Not always required for UK / Canada — verify with the consulate.
processes.notes: MOFA attestation typically follows HEC attestation; both are usually required for Germany's APS (Akademische Prüfstelle) where applicable.

processes.name: Police Clearance Certificate (PCC)
processes.description: A character certificate issued by Pakistan's police verifying that you have no criminal record. Often required as part of a student visa application, especially for scholarships requiring background checks.
steps: Visit the District Police Office or apply online (varies by province)
Provide identification and complete the PCC application form
Pay the fee and provide biometric data
Pick up the certificate (typically signed by the SSP/SP)
processes.when_required: Required by some scholarships (e.g. Chevening) and by destination consulates for certain visa categories. Always check the current visa-document checklist of your destination country.
processes.notes: PCC validity is usually 3–6 months from issue. Apply close to the visa-application date so it doesn't expire.

processes.name: Passport Issuance / Renewal
processes.description: A valid Pakistani passport is required for visa applications. Most destinations require validity beyond the intended stay (usually 6+ months past course end date).
steps: Apply online via the DGI&P / NADRA passport portal OR visit a Passport Office in person
Provide biometric data
Pay the fee (urgent / normal / fast track)
Collect the passport at the office or via courier
processes.when_required: Before any visa application. Confirm the passport is valid for the duration of your intended studies plus a buffer (usually 6 months).
processes.notes: Urgent / fast-track issuance is typically available at higher fees if the deadline is tight.

processes.name: Tuberculosis (TB) Medical Test
processes.description: A pre-visa TB screening at a designated clinic. Required for UK student visa applicants from Pakistan (stays of 6+ months) and for some other destinations.
steps: Book an appointment at an IOM-approved clinic (e.g. Gulf Medical, Islamabad / Karachi)
Attend the clinic on the appointment day
Receive the TB-clearance certificate (valid for 6 months)
processes.when_required: Required for UK student visa applications by Pakistani nationals; not currently mandatory for Canada or Germany student visas.
processes.notes: The certificate is valid for 6 months — book it close to your visa application.

processes.name: NADRA Documents (CNIC, B-Form, Birth Certificate)
processes.description: Proof-of-identity documents issued by NADRA (National Database & Registration Authority). A valid CNIC is typically required for most visa applications and Pakistani process documents.
steps: Visit the nearest NADRA Registration Centre (NRC)
Submit application with required documents
Provide biometric data
Collect the issued document
processes.when_required: Ensure your CNIC is unexpired before any official process; use Pak-Identity (online portal) for renewals where possible.
processes.notes: Renewal can usually be done online via Pak-Identity without visiting an NRC.
